findings:
Today's tape concentrates **eight 5/5 matches** in a single 72-hour news cycle, the densest convergence the validation pipeline has produced this month. The Apr 27 / Apr 28 / Apr 29 prediction trio on **Mag 7 capex disclosure** lands in unison: **MSFT prints $82.9B revenue (+18%) with Azure +40% and an explicit single-line `AI run-rate $37B (+123% YoY)` disclosure** — the first time a hyperscaler has put a per-AI-business run-rate number in the headline release as a single line item. This is one structural step short of the explicit per-token-margin gate the predictions called for, and it lands in the exact print window forecast on Apr 27-28. **Alphabet $109.9B (+22%) / Cloud $20B (+63%) / +6% AH; META -7% AH on capex shortfall + user-growth miss; AMZN mixed beat-miss; AAPL prints after-close today** complete the cycle. The Apr 29 "AI-revenue disclosure rewrite" prediction is no longer forecast: OpenAI's $8/month plan + 122M paid-subscriber projection + $100M-annualized ad pilot is the foundation-lab-side response to the WSJ Apr 28 revenue-miss tape, materializing inside one print cycle.

The **FOMC Apr 29 8-4 dissent** (highest since Oct-1992) plus **Powell's decision to remain on the Board of Governors through 2028** as a check on the Trump-admin Fed investigation is a structural surprise that sharpens the institutional-risk premium under all of these capex disclosures. Markets are now pricing forward-guidance volatility on rates separately from institutional-risk on the Fed itself — a regime change that the Apr 27-28 predictions did not anticipate but that reinforces their thesis: when the central bank's posture is contested, demand for harder AI-economics disclosures from public-cloud incumbents goes up, not down.

The AI-security thesis line lands its **single largest structural validation** in the validation pipeline's history with **MITRE ATT&CK v19 (Apr 28)**, which adds AI-agent context poisoning, tool invocation abuse, MCP server compromise, and malicious agent deployment as named techniques and splits Defense Evasion (TA0005) into Stealth + Impair Defenses. Apr 29's prediction — "agent-skills attack surface lands its own MITRE-ATT&CK-style sub-matrix" — is materializing in real-time within 24 hours of the forecast. v19 does not yet ship a dedicated skill-marketplace-poisoning sub-matrix; that is the inferred next step. **SuiteCloud Agent Skills** at SuiteConnect SF Apr 28 is the first major non-foundation-lab vendor publishing skills-as-a-product with signed-distribution as the security frame — partial materialization of the marketplace-signing leg.

The **Apr 26 / Apr 28 inference-server-CVE thesis** lifts to 5/5 on a four-CVE 48-hour cluster. **CVE-2026-40967 Spring AI FilterExpressionConverter Injection (CVSS 8.6, Apr 28)** is the first 2026 CVE squarely in the LLM-tooling supply chain — at the RAG query plane — and is structurally the same shape as the SGLang Jinja2/SSTI primitive (keys/values not properly escaped when translating filter expressions to vector-store query languages → direct injection). **CVE-2026-3854 GitHub Enterprise Server RCE (CVSS 8.7)** forces emergency patching across both GitHub.com and GHES — a single git push to any repo executes arbitrary commands. **CVE-2026-34965 Cockpit CMS authenticated RCE (CVSS 8.8)** completes the 48-hour cluster. **SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) remains unpatched 11+ days post-disclosure** with a public PoC. The model-tooling-layer-injection class is now confirmed cross-server (vLLM CVE-2025-61620 + LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 + SGLang + Spring AI). CISA KEV Apr 28 adds two with May-12 federal deadline. Pressure on a dedicated AI-Infra sub-catalog (Apr 27 prediction) tightens; the Q4 2026 forecast remains on schedule.

**Apr 25's "MCP attack-surface bottleneck → MCP Gateway / Firewall product wave"** lands at 5/5: Novita Sandbox is the first commercial agent-runtime sandbox + first MCP-Gateway-class commercial mitigation product purpose-built for OpenClaw + Hermes; Okta for AI Agents GA Apr 30 ships Agent Discovery & Governance + Agent Gateway + virtual MCP server registry; AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime adds Node.js. The product wave is actively shipping on schedule. **Apr 23's "Agent Control Plane"** prediction concentrates the same evidence on the broader category at 5/5.

**Apr 26's "Hyperscaler-AI-lab capital coupling → multi-frontier-vendor enterprise strategy"** lands at 5/5: MSFT's $37B AI run-rate disclosure is the first explicit per-AI-business single-line-item from a hyperscaler — direct precedent for the compute-stack-lock-in disclosure thesis. META's -7% AH on capex shortfall is the cleanest single-print Mag 7 reset in the cycle and sharpens the three-cloud-training-oligopoly framing; the Apr 25 training-oligopoly prediction lifts to 4/5 alongside.

**Apr 24's "1M context as default open-weight era + vector DBs redefine as long-context cache layers"** holds at 4/5: the **Spring AI FilterExpressionConverter CVE** is direct evidence the vector-DB role is being repositioned to the point its query plane has joined the inference-server-class attack surface — the architectural shift the prediction called for is now visible in CVE telemetry. PolyKV + TurboQuant + SGLang #21419/#23134 + NVIDIA kvpress + Unsloth 2026.4.8 carry-forward.

Three predictions sit at low relevance today: Apr 23's "third-party-vendor + URL-naming entropy" at 1 (no contractor / preview-URL signal; ClawHavoc + SuiteCloud Agent Skills sit on the skills-marketplace axis instead), Apr 24's "Physical-AI 8h production runs" at 2 and Apr 25's "IROS / GTC league tables" at 2 (Tesla / Figure / Boston Dynamics carry-forward without fresh production-record release). Apr 24's "capital markets reset valuation of AI-agent-displaced enterprise SaaS" sits at 3: the Mag 7 print event landed today but the crack continues to fall on the chip / foundation-lab side rather than the agent-displaced legacy-SaaS side.

The dormant entry **20260420-3 "Headless Everything"** is not yet due for periodic ping (next 2026-05-11) but Layer 1 produced one mechanical keyword hit ("Simon Willison" in Other Standout Topics). The hit was added to the validation table with the `[REVIVED]` marker per protocol, but honest relevance is 1: Willison's Apr 27-29 entries are the AGI-clause-unwind retrospective, the GPT-5.5 prompting guide, and the LLM 0.32a0 refactor — none of which discuss Headless Everything. Layer 2 semantic scan produced no match.
relation_to_my_preds:
**On "malicious local LLMs functioning like malware + zero-trust as the fundamental safeguard":** Today's tape is the strongest single-day structural validation of your thesis the pipeline has produced. **MITRE ATT&CK v19 (Apr 28)** explicitly names AI-agent context poisoning, tool invocation abuse, MCP server compromise, and malicious agent deployment as techniques — the same "internally driven multifunctional behaviors that may trigger breaches or data leakage" framing you described, now codified as a regulatory-grade attack-technique catalogue. The substrate at the moment is agent skills (ClawHavoc 1,184 malicious ClawHub skills + Cisco RSAC 2026 36% prompt-injection rate + Apr 23-29 Google +32% indirect-prompt-injection telemetry from observed payloads + 10 fresh in-the-wild IPI payloads documented for financial fraud / data destruction / API-key theft) rather than fully autonomous local LLMs, but the migration path is now visible end-to-end: skills are the unit of capability transfer, and the v19 named-technique additions are the abstraction layer the autonomous-LLM behaviors will land on next. **Okta for AI Agents GA Apr 30** ships exactly the zero-trust pattern you specified — Agent Discovery & Governance + Agent Gateway as a central MCP control plane + virtual MCP server registry + credential vaulting + auto-rotation + universal logout — i.e. "intentionally designed system paths AI alone cannot access." **Novita Sandbox (Apr 28)** is the first commercial agent-runtime sandbox purpose-built for OpenClaw + Hermes + autonomous systems with Firecracker microVM + sub-200ms cold-start + ~1s state-checkpoint resume + per-task ephemeral filesystem — sitting in the Solutions: Zero-Trust Access Control section of today's report. The behaviour-monitoring half of your prediction — "solutions that claim to monitor and manage behaviour will likely attract attention in only the short term" — continues to track: Google + Forcepoint indirect-prompt-injection in-the-wild reports plus the Help Net Security Apr 24 "indirect prompt injection is taking hold in the wild" framing show that the defensive posture cannot rely on monitoring alone. **arxiv 2604.23374 NeuroTaint** is the first comprehensive taint-tracking framework purpose-built for LLM agents — defensive scaffolding that fits the bypass-assumption caveat you flagged.

**On "cloud APIs for advanced tasks + local LLMs for daily operations, driven by SaaS price rises":** Today's tape is the strongest single-day demand-side and supply-side signal so far for both halves of your prediction. On the demand side, **MSFT $37B AI run-rate disclosure (+123% YoY)** + **Azure +40% reported beating the +37-38% cc guide** + **GOOGL Cloud $20B +63% / +6% AH** + **META -7% AH on capex shortfall + user-growth miss** + **WSJ Apr 28 OpenAI revenue-miss aftermath** + **OpenAI's response: $8/month plan + 122M paid-subscriber projection by 2026 + $100M-annualized ad pilot (projection $2.4-$2.5B 2026)** compose a coordinated picture of public markets pushing hyperscalers toward explicit per-AI-business-line disclosure — which is the leading indicator for the "service delivery models and pricing structures" change you predicted. When MSFT discloses an explicit $37B AI run-rate as a single line item, downstream SaaS pricing follows within 1-2 quarters because the per-token-margin floor becomes a published number that vendors must price against. On the supply side, the local-LLM stack continues to densify exactly as your prediction implied: **Foundry Local GA carry-forward (Apr 9, ~20MB native library, WinML execution providers)** is the missing Windows-default integration point; **Bonsai-8B 44 tok/s on iPhone 17 Pro Max / 131 tok/s on M4 Pro / 368 tok/s on RTX 4090** + **Unsloth 2026.4.8 inference 20-30% faster + Gemma-4-E2B 8 GB VRAM training** + **DeepSeek V4-Flash $0.14/$0.28 per M tokens, V4-Pro $0.145/$3.48** + **Qwen3.6-27B Apache-2.0 dense + Qwen3.6-Max-Preview** + **Kimi K2.6 first open-weight model to beat GPT-5.4 (xhigh) on SWE-Bench Pro** + **TurboQuant + SGLang #21419/#23134 + PolyKV + NVIDIA kvpress** keep building the local-side capability and per-token-cost stack. The bifurcation infrastructure is converging on schedule; the explicit consumer-facing AI-SaaS price hike has not landed today, but the leading indicator (MSFT's $37B disclosure) is now public.

**On "RL/LLM-based forecasting performance improvement becomes widespread":** Today's report carries the strongest structural-pattern signal yet via **MITRE ATT&CK v19's named-techniques wave (Apr 28)** — when the harness/protocol/skills layer is the locus of capability and the regulatory primitive is the technique catalogue, the natural research target becomes **RL on the harness and the catalogue itself** (which encompasses the future-prediction validation pipeline this scheduled task runs). **arxiv 2604.24026 "From Skill Text to Skill Structure: SSL Representation for Agent Skills" (Apr 28)** + **arxiv 2604.23374 NeuroTaint (Apr 24)** + **arxiv 2604.08407 "Your Agent Is Mine: Measuring Malicious Intermediary Attacks on the LLM Supply Chain"** + **arxiv 2604.21026 MCAP deployment-time layer profiling (Apr 22)** + the carry-forward Externalization-in-LLM-Agents framing all describe the formal substrate — skill artefacts as structured, profileable, taint-trackable, and supply-chain-auditable — that an RL-trained forecaster would optimize against. **AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime adds Node.js (Apr 28)** + **Multica v0.2.19/0.2.20** + **Hermes Agent v0.11.0** + **Anthropic Opus 4.7 GA carry-forward** provide the programmable agentic substrate. No published RL-trained-forecaster paper landed today, but the infrastructure for "RL on the forecasting harness + the agent-skills catalogue" is now actively converging on a single design surface — and the v19 named-techniques codify the failure-mode space the forecaster would be optimizing against.
